Off Calendar Aug-10-2020 Continued To Nov-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
This matter is called with Case No. PTR-18-301983; The Ernest V. Rhine Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Emily Calhoun appearing by Zoom for Kimberly Rhine Gagosian. Kevin Cholakian appearing by Zoom as Co-Counsel for Kimberly Rhine Gagosian. Aren Kavcioglu appearing by Zoom for Lidia Rhine.

Ms. Calhoun presented three issues: 1) the necessity to limit the scope of the ever morphing issues noting that there may be a need for an early motion in limine; 2) the move/need to dismiss Ronald and Jack; and 3) the issues with trial which include the fact that most of the witnesses are elderly, that they are waiting to take depositions, and that COVID has been an issue. The Court inquired as to why Ronald is named as a Respondent. Mr. Kavcioglu stated that he just associated in on this case, that at the last hearing Judge Quinn inquired as to why Ronald had not been served, and that after that hearing they personally served Ronald. Mr. Kavcioglu further stated that he does not agree with Ms. Calhoun's request to limit the scope, that he does agree with Ms. Calhoun regarding the trial issues such as the elderly witnesses, that it has been a bit challenging and may require remote depositions, that he is suggesting that this matter be set for trial in mid to late 2021, and that they will have hopefully found solutions by that time. The Court inquired about there being any chance of mediation. Mr. Kavcioglu stated that he would never turn down an opportunity to mediate. Ms. Calhoun addressed mediation, stated that the case against her client is frivolous, and that she is not authorized to accept mediation at this point. The Court stated that it wants counsel to relay to their clients what it has said here today, that it is not ready to set this matter for trial, that it would continue the petitions, and that it is encouraging mediation. Mr. Cholakian stated that he understood and that there is a key issue regarding the factual issues which should be limited to 2004 to 2005. The Court stated that a motion in limine is what you bring before the trial court, that it does not think that an early motion in limine is the vehicle for this, and that counsel should have it narrowed by years before trial. The Court continued the petitions on calendar today to November 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petitions are continued to a date after the hearing on the Motion to Withdraw Admissions. Ms. Calhoun stated that there are three pending motions and inquired about the Court wanting a joint status report from counsel. The Court stated that a joint status report is to be on file one week before November 16, 2020.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
The following cases were heard concurrently:

Appearances:
Larry Larson for Petitioner/Contestant, Lydia Rhine via Court Call.
Emilie Calhoun for Respondents Ronald Gagosian and Kimberly Gagosian.

The Court sets forth the petitions in each case currently before the court and inquired about service. Mr. Larson stated that he thought his client's petition was served, that he will serve and asked Ms. Calhoun if she will accept service on behalf of her client. Ms. Calhoun inquired as to appropriate form of service. The Court noted counsel are to work out service issues on their own and not to discuss it on the record in court. The Court noted the petitions have been pending before the court for 1 1/2 years, that matters need to be resolved by the next calling or will be ordered off calendar. Mr. Larson stated he has taken one deposition. Ms. Calhoun stated that some depositions have already been done and that she expects to take 8-12 more witness depositions of doctor(s), banker(s), petitioner, daughter, etc. The Court orders a joint status report to be filed by the next calling of case addressing claims that need an evidentiary hearing, dispute of facts, status on discovery, status on ADR, and inquired if 3 months is sufficient to counsel. Ms. Calhoun stated she wants to return sooner but do not think the case will be ready. Mr. Larson stated he is not ready for trial. The Court noted it will not delay trial for discovery, that now is the time to get diligent with discovery, that the case could get expensive, and they should negotiate between counsel.

Off Calendar Aug-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emile Calhoun for Ronald and Kimberly Gagosian (moving party) . Larry Larson for and with Lydia Rhine (respondent).

Ms. Calhoun stated that she only received contest to the tentative ruling to the motion regarding undue influence. Ms. Calhoun and Mr. Larson presented oral arguments in support of their positions.

In the related trust proceedings (PTR-18-301983), the Court granted leave to amend. Mr. Larson's amended pleading shall be filed by 9/15/18. Ms. Calhoun's demurrer shall be filed by 10/1/18. Or the alternative Ms. Calhoun's answer shall be filed by 10/15/18. Ms. Calhoun's yet to be filed demurrer (if chosen) in the trust matter is to be set on November 8,2018 at 2:00 p.m. in Dept. 204. Furthermore the August 20, 2018 hearings in the estate case are continued to November 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner Lydia Marquez Rhine's ("Lydia") Motion for Sanctions is DENIED. Respondent Kimberly Rhine Gagosian is not barred from relitigating the standing issue in the latest round of demurrer, and her challenge to the sufficiency of the "undue benefit" allegations contained in Lydia's FAWC is not so clearly frivolous or filed "primarily for an improper purpose, such as to harass or to cause unnecessary delay or needless increase in the cost of litigation" to justify awarding sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.7.

The demurrer to first amended will contest and grounds of objection to probate of will of Ernest V. Rhine on the grounds of undue influence is taken under submission.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Larry Larson for Petitioner.
Emilie Calhoun for Kim Gagosian (Respondent).

The Court asks whether the objector intends to file a petition to probate the will. Ms. Calhoun stated that there are no assets to be probated, that she does not see a need to appoint anybody and that she received notice that the petitioner is contesting the Trust amendments in another County. Ms. Calhoun also suggested denying the petition or putting it out for six months. The Court stated that it can not deny the petition and that the petitioner has the right to have the opportunity to file a will contest. Ms. Calhoun stated that she will file her petition. Mr. Larson stated that his Client will be eighty in a couple of months, that his Client received notice to vacate the premises and suggested fourteen days for Ms. Calhoun to file their petition for probate. Mr. Larson also stated that there are bank accounts, vehicles and time shares, and that they have the right to examine those assets. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to October 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing of Ms. Calhoun's petition.
